questionText: Gravida
options: A woman who has never been pregnant
A woman who has had two or more pregnancies
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Nulligravida
options: A woman who has never been pregnant
A woman who has had two or more pregnancies
correctAnswer: A woman who has never been pregnant
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Multigravida
options: A woman who has never been pregnant
A woman who has had two or more pregnancies
correctAnswer: A woman who has had two or more pregnancies
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Term Pregnancy
options: A pregnancy that goes beyond 42 weeks
Pregnancy that is at least 20 weeks to 37 weeks
Capacity to live outside uterus, around 22-25 weeks
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Post Term
options: A pregnancy that goes beyond 42 weeks
Pregnancy that is at least 20 weeks to 37 weeks
Capacity to live outside uterus, around 22-25 weeks
correctAnswer: A pregnancy that goes beyond 42 weeks
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Preterm
options: A pregnancy that goes beyond 42 weeks
Pregnancy that is at least 20 weeks to 37 weeks
Capacity to live outside uterus, around 22-25 weeks
correctAnswer: Pregnancy that is at least 20 weeks to 37 weeks
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Viability
options: A pregnancy that goes beyond 42 weeks
Pregnancy that is at least 20 weeks to 37 weeks
Capacity to live outside uterus, around 22-25 weeks
correctAnswer: Capacity to live outside uterus, around 22-25 weeks
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Congenital
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Genetic
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Estimated Date of Delivery (EDD) / Estimated Date of Confinement (EDC) Calculation
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Presumptive (Subjective) Signs of Pregnancy
options: Nausea/vomiting, Change in breast sensations/size, Increased urinary frequency, Fatigue, Missed menstrual period (Amenorrhea)

Enlargement of the uterus/abdomen, Positive test for hCG in the maternal urine or blood, Softening and compressibility of lower uterus (Hegar sign), Bluish or cyanotic color of cervix and upper vagina (Chadwick sign), Softening of the cervix (Goodell sign)

Detection of fetal heart tones by auscultation, ultrasonography, or a Doppler,  Measuring of fundal height, Palpation of fetal body parts using Leopold maneuvers, Objective detection of fetal movements (Quickening!), Radiological or ultrasonographic demonstration of fetal parts
correctAnswer: Nausea/vomiting, Change in breast sensations/size, Increased urinary frequency, Fatigue, Missed menstrual period (Amenorrhea)
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Probable (Objective) Signs of Pregnancy
options: Nausea/vomiting, Change in breast sensations/size, Increased urinary frequency, Fatigue, Missed menstrual period (Amenorrhea)

Enlargement of the uterus/abdomen, Positive test for hCG in the maternal urine or blood, Softening and compressibility of lower uterus (Hegar sign), Bluish or cyanotic color of cervix and upper vagina (Chadwick sign), Softening of the cervix (Goodell sign)

Detection of fetal heart tones by auscultation, ultrasonography, or a Doppler,  Measuring of fundal height, Palpation of fetal body parts using Leopold maneuvers, Objective detection of fetal movements (Quickening!), Radiological or ultrasonographic demonstration of fetal parts
correctAnswer: Enlargement of the uterus/abdomen, Positive test for hCG in the maternal urine or blood, Softening and compressibility of lower uterus (Hegar sign), Bluish or cyanotic color of cervix and upper vagina (Chadwick sign), Softening of the cervix (Goodell sign)
explanationText: n/a

questionText: Positive (Actual) Signs of Pregnancy
options: Nausea/vomiting, Change in breast sensations/size, Increased urinary frequency, Fatigue, Missed menstrual period (Amenorrhea)

Enlargement of the uterus/abdomen, Positive test for hCG in the maternal urine or blood, Softening and compressibility of lower uterus (Hegar sign), Bluish or cyanotic color of cervix and upper vagina (Chadwick sign), Softening of the cervix (Goodell sign)

Detection of fetal heart tones by auscultation, ultrasonography, or a Doppler,  Measuring of fundal height, Palpation of fetal body parts using Leopold maneuvers, Objective detection of fetal movements (Quickening!), Radiological or ultrasonographic demonstration of fetal parts
correctAnswer: Detection of fetal heart tones by auscultation, ultrasonography, or a Doppler,  Measuring of fundal height, Palpation of fetal body parts using Leopold maneuvers, Objective detection of fetal movements (Quickening!), Radiological or ultrasonographic demonstration of fetal parts
explanationText: n/a

questionText: TORCH Screen
options: Toxoplamosis, Other/Hepatitis B, Rubella, Provide rubella vaccine if pre-conception (do not give during pregnancy and avoid pregnancy for 3 months post vaccine!), Cytomegalovirus, Herpes simplex
correctAnswer: Toxoplamosis, Other/Hepatitis B, Rubella, Provide rubella vaccine if pre-conception (do not give during pregnancy and avoid pregnancy for 3 months post vaccine!), Cytomegalovirus, Herpes simplex
explanationText: n/a

questionText: The Embryotic period lasts for how many days/weeks?
explanationText: n/a

questionText: A woman has been pregnant 4 times and 1 miscarriage. Her first child was born at 37 weeks, second child born at 41 weeks, and third child born at 42 weeks. All of her children are living. A urine pregnancy test indicates that she is pregnant. What is her GTPAL?
explanationText: Gravida: woman who is pregnant/number of pregnancies including this one, Term: 37-42 weeks, Preterm: 20-37 weeks, Abortions/Miscarriages: elective vs. spontaneous, Living children: currently

questionText: Client last menstrual cycle started on January 27th and is unsure when it ended. Her EDD is:
explanationText: -3 months, +7 days

questionText: A client who is 7 weeks of gestation is experiencing nausea and vomiting in the morning. Which of the following information should the nurse include in the teaching?
options: Eat crackers of plain toast before getting out of bed.
Awaken during the night to eat a snack
Skip breakfast and each lunch after nausea has subsided.
correctAnswer: Eat crackers of plain toast before getting out of bed.
explanationText: Nausea and vomiting in early pregnancy (often called morning sickness) is common around 6–12 weeks and is linked to hormonal changes, especially increases in human chorionic gonadotropin and estrogen. Eating dry, bland foods (like crackers or toast) before getting out of bed helps: Prevent an empty stomach, which can worsen nausea, Stabilize blood sugar levels, Reduce gastric irritation first thing in the morning

questionText: A nurse is teaching a client who is at 23 weeks of gestation about immunizations. Which of the following statements should the nurse include in the teaching?
options: You should not receive the rubella vaccine while breastfeeding.
You should receive a varicella vaccine before you deliver.
You can receive an influenza vaccination during pregnancy.
You cannot receive the Tdap vaccine until after you deliver.
correctAnswer: You can receive an influenza vaccination during pregnancy.
explanationText: The inactivated influenza vaccine is safe and recommended during pregnancy at any gestational age, including at 23 weeks. It helps protect: The mother from severe illness The fetus/newborn, since antibodies cross the placenta

questionText: A nurse is preparing to measure the fundal height of a client who is at 22 weeks of gestation. At which location should the nurse expect to palpate the fundus?
explanationText: Fundal height helps estimate gestational age by measuring the distance from the pubic symphysis to the top of the uterus (fundus). A useful rule: Around 20 weeks, the fundus is at the level of the umbilicus. After 20 weeks, it rises about 1 cm per week. So at 22 weeks, the fundus should be slightly above the umbilicus.

questionText: TORCH infections include all of the following except:
explanationText: TORCH stands for Toxoplasmosis, Other (e.g., syphilis), Rubella, Cytomegalovirus, and Herpes simplex. Toxemia (preeclampsia) is a maternal condition, not a congenital infection.

questionText: A client at 12 weeks’ gestation reports heartburn. Which explanation is correct?
options: Increased progesterone causes decreased smooth muscle motility
Pressure from the growing uterus displaces the stomach
Retained bile in the liver results in delayed digestion
Increased estrogen causes increased secretion of hydrochloric acid
correctAnswer: Increased progesterone causes decreased smooth muscle motility
explanationText: Progesterone relaxes smooth muscle, slowing gastrointestinal motility and relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter. This leads to reflux and heartburn. Uterine pressure is not significant at 12 weeks.

questionText: When completing a skin assessment of a client in the second trimester, the nurse should expect to find:
explanationText: Chloasma (melasma or 'mask of pregnancy') is a normal finding caused by hormonal changes that increase melanin production, leading to facial hyperpigmentation.

questionText: Which of the following is a probable sign of pregnancy?
options: Visualization of the fetus via ultrasound
explanationText: Probable signs are objective but not definitive (e.g., uterine enlargement, positive pregnancy test). Positive signs confirm pregnancy (e.g., ultrasound visualization). Presumptive signs include breast changes.

questionText: The frequency of contractions is measured:
options: From the beginning of one contraction to the beginning of the next
From the end of one contraction to the beginning of the next
From the beginning to the end of the same contraction
By measuring the mmHg of the contraction
correctAnswer: From the beginning of one contraction to the beginning of the next
explanationText: Frequency is measured from the start of one contraction to the start of the next. Duration is measured from beginning to end of the same contraction.

questionText: A nurse is assessing a client in active labor and notes the presenting part is at 0 station. What does this indicate?
options: The fetal head is in the left occiput posterior position
The lowermost portion of the fetus is at the level of the ischial spines
correctAnswer: The lowermost portion of the fetus is at the level of the ischial spines
explanationText: Station refers to the position of the presenting part relative to the ischial spines. At 0 station, the fetus is engaged at the level of the ischial spines.

questionText: A multigravida who is 8 cm dilated and 100% effaced is in which phase of labor?
explanationText: The transition phase occurs from 8–10 cm dilation and is the most intense part of the first stage of labor, with strong, frequent contractions.

questionText: There are four stages of labor.
explanationText: Stage 1: 0–10 cm dilation (latent, active, transition). Stage 2: pushing and delivery of the baby. Stage 3: delivery of the placenta. Stage 4: immediate postpartum recovery (first 1–4 hours).

questionText: Which finding is abnormal in a newborn?
explanationText: Normal newborn heart rate is 110–160 bpm. A rate of 180 bpm is tachycardia. The other values fall within normal newborn ranges.

questionText: Which term describes cyanosis that is normal during the first few days of a newborn’s life?
explanationText: Acrocyanosis (bluish hands and feet) is normal in newborns due to immature circulation. Central cyanosis is abnormal and indicates hypoxia.

questionText: The stool of breastfed infants:
explanationText: Breastfed stools are typically yellow, loose, and seedy. Meconium (first stool) is thick, dark, and sticky.

questionText: Safe sleep for infants should include:
explanationText: Safe sleep reduces the risk of SIDS. Infants should sleep on a firm surface with no loose bedding, pillows, or bumper pads.

questionText: Which finding indicates newborn respiratory distress?
explanationText: Grunting is a sign of respiratory distress, indicating the newborn is trying to keep alveoli open. Other signs include nasal flaring, retractions, and tachypnea. Acrocyanosis and RR of 56 can be normal.

questionText: Infants should regain or exceed their birth weight by:
explanationText: Newborns typically lose 5–10% of their birth weight after delivery but should regain it by 10–14 days of life.

questionText: Culture is best defined as:
options: A pattern of shared attitudes, beliefs, norms, roles, and values
correctAnswer: A pattern of shared attitudes, beliefs, norms, roles, and values
explanationText: Culture refers to shared attitudes, beliefs, self-definitions, norms, roles, and values among a group of people.

questionText: Race is best described as:
options: A social construct associated with power
correctAnswer: A social construct associated with power
explanationText: Race is a social construct used by societies to categorize people based on perceived physical and behavioral traits.

questionText: Ethnicity refers to:
options: A shared identity and cultural uniqueness
correctAnswer: A shared identity and cultural uniqueness
explanationText: Ethnicity focuses on shared identity, values, meanings, and ways of living that distinguish a group.

questionText: A minority group is defined as:
options: A group with the largest population
A group with equal power in society
A group disadvantaged in power, control, and wealth
correctAnswer: A group disadvantaged in power, control, and wealth
explanationText: Minority groups are those who experience disadvantages in power, control over their lives, and access to wealth.

questionText: Sex refers to:
options: A person's biological status assigned at birth
correctAnswer: A person's biological status assigned at birth
explanationText: Sex is the biological classification (male or female) assigned at birth.

questionText: Gender identity is defined as:
options: A person's internal sense of being male, female, both, or neither
correctAnswer: A person's internal sense of being male, female, both, or neither
explanationText: Gender identity is an individual’s internal understanding of their gender, which may differ from their assigned sex.

questionText: Gender expression refers to:
options: A person's outward presentation of gender
correctAnswer: A person's outward presentation of gender
explanationText: Gender expression is how a person externally expresses their gender identity through behavior, clothing, and appearance.

questionText: Sexual orientation is defined as:
options: A person's romantic, emotional, or sexual attraction to others
correctAnswer: A person's romantic, emotional, or sexual attraction to others
explanationText: Sexual orientation refers to who a person is attracted to emotionally, romantically, or sexually.

questionText: What does the 'C' in the FICA tool stand for?
explanationText: The FICA tool includes Faith, Importance, Community, and Address for spiritual assessment.

questionText: Spirituality is best defined as:
options: A sense of peace, purpose, and connection
correctAnswer: A sense of peace, purpose, and connection
explanationText: Spirituality involves a person’s sense of meaning, purpose, and connection to others or a higher power.

questionText: Which of the following is part of culturally competent care?
options: Self-awareness of one's own cultural identity
Treating all patients exactly the same without consideration
correctAnswer: Self-awareness of one's own cultural identity
explanationText: Culturally competent care includes self-awareness, knowledge, skill, and a desire to understand others.

questionText: A woman at 16 weeks gestation is in which trimester?
explanationText: The second trimester spans from weeks 13 to 27, so 16 weeks falls within this period.

questionText: What does 'multigravida' mean?
options: This is not her first pregnancy
She has never been pregnant before
correctAnswer: This is not her first pregnancy
explanationText: Multigravida refers to a woman who has been pregnant more than once.

questionText: What is the ideal time to begin regular prenatal visits?
options: When the menstrual period is 3 weeks late
As soon as possible after the first missed menstrual period
correctAnswer: As soon as possible after the first missed menstrual period
explanationText: Early prenatal care is important for monitoring maternal and fetal health.

questionText: Susie's LMP was 7/16/2021. What is her estimated date of delivery?
explanationText: Using Naegele’s rule: add 7 days, subtract 3 months, and add 1 year to the LMP.

questionText: Which prenatal care activities are appropriate at 16 weeks gestation? (Select all that apply)
options: Send a blood sample for alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) testing
Advise the client to have DNA testing
correctAnswer: Provide nutrition education, Obtain a midstream urinalysis, Send a blood sample for alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) testing, Complete an obstetrical ultrasound
explanationText: At 16 weeks, routine care includes education, urinalysis, AFP screening, and ultrasound. DNA testing is not routinely advised unless indicated.

questionText: Which finding in a 16-week pregnant client requires follow-up?
explanationText: A slightly elevated blood pressure in pregnancy may require monitoring for hypertensive disorders.

questionText: During the second trimester, prenatal visits are typically scheduled every:
explanationText: Routine prenatal visits during the second trimester occur every 4 weeks.

questionText: An ultrasound in pregnancy is primarily used to assess fetal:
explanationText: Ultrasound evaluates fetal anatomy to detect possible anomalies.

questionText: Fetal movement is typically first felt by the mother around:
explanationText: Quickening, or first fetal movement, is usually felt around 16–20 weeks.

questionText: Fundal height measurement is used to assess:
explanationText: Fundal height is a noninvasive way to estimate fetal growth during pregnancy.

questionText: Fundal height measurements begin routinely after:
explanationText: After 20 weeks, fundal height correlates with gestational age and is measured at each visit.

questionText: Rho(D) immune globulin is given after birth when:
explanationText: RhoGAM prevents Rh sensitization when an Rh-negative mother delivers an Rh-positive infant.

questionText: Which of the following is a sign of true labor?
options: Increasing intensity and frequency of contractions
correctAnswer: Increasing intensity and frequency of contractions
explanationText: True labor involves contractions that become stronger, longer, and closer together.

questionText: What does 'station -2' indicate?
options: Baby is above the ischial spines
Baby is at the ischial spines
correctAnswer: Baby is above the ischial spines
explanationText: Negative stations indicate the fetal head is above the ischial spines.

questionText: Which stage of labor involves pushing and delivery of the baby?
explanationText: Stage 2 begins at full dilation (10 cm) and ends with delivery of the baby.

questionText: Which stage of labor includes delivery of the placenta?
explanationText: Stage 3 is the period after birth of the baby until delivery of the placenta.

questionText: What does AROM stand for?
explanationText: AROM is when the provider intentionally ruptures the amniotic sac.

questionText: Apgar score evaluates which of the following? (Select all that apply)
correctAnswer: Heart rate, Respiratory effort, Muscle tone, Color
explanationText: Apgar assesses heart rate, respirations, muscle tone, reflex irritability, and color.

questionText: Which maternal position improves uterine blood flow during labor?
explanationText: The left lateral position improves blood flow and oxygenation to the fetus.

questionText: A newborn respiratory rate of 40 breaths per minute is:
explanationText: Normal newborn respiratory rate is 30–60 breaths per minute.

questionText: Which is a sign of respiratory distress in a newborn?
explanationText: Nasal flaring, grunting, and retractions are signs of respiratory distress.

questionText: Newborns typically lose what percentage of birth weight in the first few days?
explanationText: It is normal for newborns to lose 5–10% of their birth weight initially.

questionText: Which intervention is appropriate immediately after birth?
explanationText: Skin-to-skin promotes bonding, temperature regulation, and breastfeeding success.

questionText: Which finding in a newborn requires further evaluation?
explanationText: Pale mucous membranes may indicate poor perfusion or anemia and require follow-up.
